dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4575 IS: Supporting Mental Assistance Responder Teams Community Policing Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Bennet introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to provide funding for innovations in community policing, mental health care, and community safety, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Supporting Mental Assistance Responder Teams Community Policing Act or the SMART Community Policing Act.
The purpose of this Act is to strengthen community policing programs to—

paragraph.text: de-escalate interactions with law enforcement officers to achieve better outcomes for non-violent individuals experiencing crisis or trauma relating to mental health issues, poverty, homelessness, and substance use disorders;
build collaborative partnerships to connect individuals with mental health services and community resources; and
produce better outcomes for communities and law enforcement officers by delivering the appropriate treatment and other support services to individuals in need.

section.header: Additional authorized uses of COPS funds
section.text: Section 1701(b) of title I of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 (34 U.S.C. 10381(b)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (24), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (25), by striking the period at the end and inserting a semicolon; and
by adding at the end the following:
to establish or expand a mobile crisis team program to—

subparagraph.text: hire skilled mental health professionals and paramedics to—

subclause.text: certain 911 dispatch calls at the request of law enforcement officers; and

clause.text: stabilize encounters between law enforcement officers and individuals experiencing a mental or behavioral health crisis; and
assume responsibility for securing mental health services for individuals, including individuals in crisis who may need further evaluation and treatment;

subparagraph.text: train law enforcement officers partnering with mental health professionals and paramedics;
use a mobile unit to facilitate the response of law enforcement officers and mental health professionals and paramedics to community members experiencing a mental or behavioral health crisis; and

paragraph.text: to establish or expand a co-responder program under which—

subparagraph.text: a trained law enforcement officer is paired with a behavioral health clinician or paramedic to—

clause.text: de-escalate situations involving a mental health crisis;
connect individuals with mental illness to appropriate services; and
provide other effective and efficient responses to individuals with mental illness; and

subparagraph.text: additional personnel, including law enforcement officers and case managers, may be hired; and

paragraph.text: to establish or expand a case management and outreach team—

subparagraph.text: to follow up with individuals experiencing a mental or behavioral health crisis to—

clause.text: connect those individuals with mental health services and community resources; and
help those individuals abide by treatment plans and meet other responsibilities, such as work, school, and training;

subparagraph.text: to develop specific solutions for, and provide support resources to, individuals who frequently use emergency services to reduce repeat interactions between the individuals described in subparagraph (A) and law enforcement officers or mental health professionals and paramedics; and
which may be established as a part of a mobile crisis team program under paragraph (26), a co-responder program under paragraph (27), or an independent team.
